items.title: Knowledge graph for RAG
items.scenario: Entities (people, products, papers) are vertices; relations (`wrote`, `cites`, `manages`) are edges.
items.explanation: Traversals expand context around a query entity; shortest path explains why two entities are related.

items.title: Agent / tool orchestration (LangGraph-style)
items.scenario: An agent's flow is a graph: input node → tool A → decision → tool B → output.
items.explanation: DAGs allow safe execution order; back-edges (cycles) let the agent retry or refine until a condition is met.

items.title: HNSW vector index
items.scenario: A vector store finds approximate nearest neighbours by greedy graph search across layers.
items.explanation: HNSW IS a graph — each vector is a node, edges link to similar neighbours. Search is a directed walk towards the query.

items.title: Citation graph
items.scenario: Each paper cites others; build a directed graph and find influence clusters.
items.explanation: PageRank, BFS from a seed paper, and SCC detection all become graph algorithms.

items.title: Dependency resolver
items.scenario: A Python project's packages depend on others; install order must respect dependencies.
items.explanation: Topological sort over the dependency DAG gives a safe install order; a cycle signals an unresolvable conflict.
